Code fragment: private static final String SQL_SELECT_DATASET = "select * from " + DATASET + " where code = :datasetCode";
Explanation: Using SELECT * fetches all columns from the DATASET table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and break if the table schema changes. Replace with an explicit column list specifying only the columns needed for the export.

Fetching all columns into WordRecord objects retrieves every column from the word table, even though only 'value' and 'valueAsWord' fields are used. Consider creating a projection that selects only the required columns (id, value, valueAsWord) to reduce memory usage and improve query performance.

Using LIKE with wildcards (% and _) for searching word values is inefficient for full-text search scenarios. Consider using PostgreSQL's full-text search with tsvector/tsquery or a dedicated search engine for better performance and relevance ranking.
